title: allium cupuliferum var. regelii
intro: Allium cupuliferum var. regelii, also known as the Regel's Allium or Onion-flowering Onion, is a plant species in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its small, bell-shaped flowers and onion-like scent. Traditionally, the plant's bulbs have been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments due to their antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Allium cupuliferum var. regelii include reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin infections. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and topical applications, although the specific preparations and dosages should be determined by a qualified healthcare professional.
